# Preface {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| include: false
source("_common.R")
```

This site reproduces, in **R**, the main empirical results of

> Lusk, J. L., & Schroeder, T. C. (2004). *Are Choice Experiments Incentive
> Compatible? A Test with Quality Differentiated Beef Steaks.* American Journal
> of Agricultural Economics, **86**(2), 467–482.

The original analysis was conducted in **LIMDEP**. This is a replication project
for **AGEC 5045 — Applied Discrete Choice Analysis (NTU, 114-2)**, written as a
Quarto book so that **code, results, and commentary** sit side by side and the
whole analysis can be reproduced end to end.

## What is reproduced

| Output | Chapter | Method |
|---|---|---|
| Data preparation | [Data](02-data.qmd) | read `harrison.xls`, reshape to choice format |
| Tables 1–2 | [Descriptives](03-descriptives.qmd) | demographics & choice frequencies |
| Table 3 | [MNL](04-mnl.qmd) | multinomial logit + Swait–Louviere scale test |
| IIA test | [IIA](05-iia.qmd) | universal ("mother") logit LR test |
| Table 4 | [Advanced models](06-advanced-models.qmd) | HEV, RPL (MNP discussed) |
| Table 5 & Figure 2 | [WTP](07-wtp-figure.qmd) | willingness to pay + Poe test; HEV market shares |
| Discussion | [Discrepancies](08-discrepancies.qmd) | replication experience & gaps |

## How to read it

* **Data preparation runs live** in the book — the [Data](02-data.qmd) chapter
  reads the raw spreadsheet and builds the estimation dataset in front of you.
* **Model estimation code is shown but loaded, not re-run.** The heavy models
  (custom-likelihood MNL scale test, HEV quadrature, simulated-ML RPL, the Poe
  bootstrap) are estimated by the numbered scripts in `R/`, saved to
  `output/*.rds`, and loaded here; results are then recomputed for display. This
  keeps the book fast and deterministic while still showing every line of code.
* **Commentary is set off in coloured callout boxes** so my own notes are never
  confused with the paper's claims or the raw output.

::: {.callout-note}
## Reproducibility
All code and data are in the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/JunJie-Chang/ADCA-replication).
To rebuild from scratch, run the scripts in `R/` in order (see
[Reproducibility](09-reproducibility.qmd)) and re-render the book.
:::

The headline result, stated once up front: **hypothetical choices overstate
real willingness to pay.** Every qualitative finding of the paper replicates;
the quantitative gaps that remain are documented and interpreted in the final
chapter.
